_comment: Template for paper summaries. Use _comment fields for documentation.

Basics._title_guidance: Exact title as it appears on the paper.
Basics._code_url_guidance: Link to official code repository if available. Check paper footnotes, abstract, or appendix.
Basics._venue_guidance: Only state what the paper explicitly claims (e.g., in comments or header). If unverifiable, use 'arXiv preprint'.
Basics._code_availability_guidance: REQUIRED. One of: 'publicly available' (+ URL), 'not yet released' (paper promises but unavailable), 'not provided' (no mention). Also note any unreleased artifacts: prompt templates, evaluation scripts, trained model weights.

Core._comment: High-level summary accessible to beginners. Avoid jargon in one_sentence_thesis.

topic_relevance._topics_guidance: Research areas this paper belongs to. Use standard terms from the field.
topic_relevance._primary_topic_guidance: The single most relevant research area.
topic_relevance._sub_topic_guidance: Specific sub-areas within the primary topic.
topic_relevance._primary_focus_guidance: List specific aspects the paper addresses within the sub-topic.

Core._one_sentence_thesis_guidance: MUST be under 30 words. No jargon. Explain the core idea in plain language.

core_problem._problem_statement_guidance: 1-2 sentences describing the core challenge. Be specific about what fails and why.
core_problem._why_it_matters_guidance: Include: (1) real-world impact, (2) research gaps in prior work. Provide plain-language explanations alongside any technical references.
core_problem._concrete_example_guidance: REQUIRED. A specific input/output scenario showing what goes wrong with current approaches. If the paper includes a case study, use it. Example: 'When asked [question], an independently-trained rewriter produces [bad output] because [reason], while the proposed approach produces [good output].'

key_novelty._main_idea_guidance: Short name for the contribution: e.g., 'Cooperative Multi-Agent RL for Modular RAG (MMOA-RAG)'
key_novelty._explanation_guidance: 2-3 bullets explaining HOW the method works at INTUITION/ANALOGY level ONLY. Do NOT include formal notation, loss functions, or algorithmic specifics here—those belong exclusively in Technical_details. The last bullet should bridge to Technical_details (e.g., 'The technical formulation as a multi-agent RL problem is detailed below').

Core._evaluation_highlights_guidance: 2-3 most impressive results only. Include relative improvement (%). Detailed results go in Experiments.key_results.
Core._breakthrough_guidance: Score must be consistent with novelty claims. If claiming 'first to do X', score should reflect significance.

Technical_details._comment: Technical details for expert readers. Include model sizes, hyperparameters, compute requirements. Do NOT repeat analogies or intuitive explanations already given in Core.key_novelty; provide the formal specification here.

problem_definition._comment: Formal problem setting - what are we solving?
problem_definition._inputs_guidance: What the system receives: e.g., 'Natural language question q'
problem_definition._outputs_guidance: What the system produces: e.g., 'Predicted answer Ans_predict'
problem_definition._constraints_guidance: Key assumptions or constraints: e.g., 'Fixed retriever, K=10 documents, F1 as reward'

prerequisite_knowledge._comment: Prerequisite knowledge and key terms users should know to understand this paper.
prerequisite_knowledge._prerequisites_guidance: List concepts the reader should know to understand this paper.

key_terms._comment: REQUIRED: Define ALL technical terms, acronyms, and method names used ANYWHERE in the entire summary. After drafting the summary, perform a MANDATORY POST-DRAFT SCAN: check every section individually (Core.one_sentence_thesis, evaluation_highlights, core_problem, Technical_details, Experiments.key_results notes, Figures descriptions and key_insights). HIGH-VISIBILITY RULE: Terms appearing in one_sentence_thesis, evaluation_highlights, or figure key_insights MUST be defined—these are the first things readers see. Common terms easy to miss: PPO, DPO, MARL, dense retrieval, silver labels, cosine schedule, clipped surrogate objective, Top-k, greedy decoding, beam search, logit, ViT, CLIP, Pareto improvement, HCI, BLEU, ROUGE, perplexity, attention heads. If in doubt, define it.

_example.RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation—AI systems that answer questions by first searching for relevant documents
_example.F1 score: A metric balancing precision (are answers correct?) and recall (are answers complete?)
_example.PPO: Proximal Policy Optimization—a reinforcement learning algorithm that updates a policy in small, stable steps using a clipped objective
_example.MARL: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning—RL setting where multiple agents learn simultaneously in a shared environment

system_pipeline._comment: Only show e2e pipeline for the main INFERENCE task. NOT for data generation or training.
system_pipeline._functional_grouping_guidance: When the pipeline has >5 modules, group them by function. Add a 'functional_group' field to each module (e.g., 'Input Processing', 'Retrieval & Selection', 'Generation'). List groups in high_level_flow as: 'Group Name: module1 → module2'.

modules._model_guidance: Specify exact architecture: 'LLaMA-2-7B with LoRA adapters' not 'LLaMA-based'
modules._inputs_guidance: Include context length limits: 'Original question (max 128 tokens)'

system_pipeline._novel_elements_guidance: List genuinely new design choices introduced by this paper, not standard techniques.

model_architecture._comment: Specify exact model variants, sizes, and training details, for the major model

training_details._comment: Only if paper uses post-training (fine-tuning, RL, etc.)
training_details._warm_start_guidance: Describe initialization procedure if any SFT/pretraining before main training
training_details._hyperparameters_guidance: For RL: include clip_epsilon, gae_lambda, entropy_coefficient, value_coefficient. Always include learning_rate, batch_size, epochs. Only include values explicitly stated in the paper.
training_details._compute_guidance: REQUIRED field. GPU type/count, training time, inference latency. If not reported in the paper, state 'Not reported in the paper'. Do NOT omit this field.

optimization_details._comment: Only if paper uses optimization without post-training
optimization_details._approximation_guidance: Only if applicable: 'DQN (Q-learning), DDPG (deep deterministic policy gradient)', etc.

comparison_to_sota._comment: Compare to SOTA methods beyond just the baselines in the paper.
comparison_to_sota._related_methods_guidance: Include brief description: 'RetRobust: Single-module RL optimizing retriever only'
comparison_to_sota._differences_guidance: Format: 'vs. [Method]: [specific difference]'. BROADER POSITIONING: Include at least one comparison to a technique the paper does NOT cite but that addresses a similar problem from the wider literature. Example: if the paper uses multi-agent RL, compare to joint SFT and DPO alternatives; if attention manipulation, compare to activation steering.

Limitations._comment: What remaining problems this paper cannot solve? Top-level section parallel to Core, Experiments, etc.
Limitations._acknowledged_guidance: Limitations the paper itself states explicitly.
Limitations._potential_issues_guidance: Issues you identify that the paper does not discuss (e.g., scalability, generalization concerns).
Limitations._scope_guidance: What the paper explicitly does NOT cover (languages, domains, tasks).

Experiments._comment: Experimental setup and quantitative results. NEVER use TODO placeholders in any field.

evaluation_setup._eval_setting_guidance: High-level description of how evaluation is done: e.g., 'Open-domain QA with retrieval from Wikipedia'.

benchmarks._task_type_guidance: What kind of task: e.g., 'Multi-hop reasoning QA', 'Single-hop ambiguous QA'.

evaluation_setup._metrics_guidance: List ALL metrics the paper reports, not just the primary one.
evaluation_setup._stats_guidance: Only include if paper explicitly reports statistical tests. If not reported, state 'Not explicitly reported in the paper'. Do NOT fabricate.

Experiments._key_results_organization_guidance: When there are >5 entries, group them by result_type and insert a 'result_group_narrative' entry before each group explaining what the group demonstrates. Example: {"result_group_narrative": "The following ablation results isolate each agent's contribution to the full pipeline."}

key_results._comment: One entry per significant result. NEVER use TODO for any field. If a value is unavailable, use 'Not reported in the paper' or omit the entry entirely.
key_results._baseline_guidance: Name the specific method AND verify it is the actual best baseline for this metric from the paper's table.
key_results._baseline_value_guidance: Must be an actual number from the paper. If not available, use 'See [Table X] in paper'.
key_results._paper_value_guidance: Must be an actual number from the paper. If not available, use 'See [Table X] in paper'.
key_results._delta_guidance: Must equal paper_value minus baseline_value. ARITHMETIC CHECK: verify paper_value − baseline_value = delta before finalizing. If baseline_value or paper_value is not a number, describe qualitatively.
key_results._ci_guidance: Only include if paper reports CIs. If not reported, omit this field entirely. Do NOT fabricate.

Experiments._takeaways_guidance: Include both absolute improvements and relative percentages (e.g., '+1.8 F1, ~4% relative improvement')

architecture_figure._comment: MUST be system architecture/workflow diagram, NOT results plots or tables. Prioritize depicting the paper's CORE mechanism, not auxiliary data pipelines.
architecture_figure._layout_guidance: Describe spatial arrangement: 'Left-to-right flow: Input → Module A → Module B → Output'
architecture_figure._visual_detail_guidance: REQUIRED details: (1) color coding/shading conventions (e.g., 'blue = trainable, gray = frozen'); (2) which components are trainable vs. fixed/frozen; (3) arrow semantics (solid = data flow, dashed = gradient/reward, etc.). If color coding is not discernible, note 'Color coding not discernible'.
architecture_figure._reading_guidance: Help readers parse the figure: 'Solid arrows show inference flow; dashed arrows show training signals'

major_experiment_figures._comment: Figures (NOT tables) showing key experimental results. Include diagnostic/analysis figures (attention maps, convergence curves, error breakdowns) if present.
